strengths: Direct match on primary stack: TypeScript, Node.js, and React used across multiple recent senior roles including current position at re:cinq
AI agent infrastructure experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement

Polyglot background spanning 15+ years with cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker), multiple databases (MongoDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL-adjacent), and both frontend and backend — covers the full required technical surface
concerns: NestJS is listed as a primary stack requirement but is absent from the resume across all roles and skills sections
Kotlin/Java backend work (secondary stack) is not evidenced in any recent role; only a brief Java mention in robotic-arm work from 2016–2018

Testing expertise is demonstrated through D-language tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) rather than the required frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright), leaving alignment with the specified testing stack unverified
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist fullstack engineer whose current role at re:cinq closely mirrors the target position — TypeScript/Node.js product work, AI agent tooling, and internal developer infrastructure — giving him high relevance to the core of the role. The main gap is NestJS, which is listed as a primary framework but does not appear anywhere in his history; given his demonstrated ability to pick up new frameworks across a 15-year polyglot career this is a learnable gap rather than a disqualifying one, but it should be probed in interview. Kotlin/Java depth is thin for the secondary stack requirement, and the testing framework alignment is indirect. Overall the candidate's senior-level fullstack breadth, AI agent credibility, and direct stack overlap make him a competitive fit, contingent on confirming NestJS readiness.
